name: Meroxa Data Infrastructure API
what_it_does: Cloud data streaming platform with REST API for building real-time data pipelines via managed connectors — primarily built around Conduit (open-source pipeline engine) with a hosted platform for connecting sources to destinations.
use_cases: Building real-time streaming pipelines between databases and data warehouses
Deploying Conduit-based pipelines as managed services via REST API
Monitoring pipeline throughput and connector health for automation agents
Creating data streaming applications with JavaScript/WASM plugin transforms
Moving data between databases, queues, and object stores in real-time
not_for: Batch ETL without streaming needs (use Fivetran or Airbyte)
Enterprise CDC at massive scale (use Debezium or Confluent)
Organizations needing extensive connector catalog (Fivetran has more connectors)
best_when: An agent manages streaming pipelines using Conduit-based connectors, especially for teams preferring open-source pipeline runtime with managed cloud hosting.
avoid_when: You need a large connector catalog (200+) or enterprise SLAs — Meroxa/Conduit ecosystem is smaller than Airbyte or Fivetran.

auth.notes: API key bearer tokens from Meroxa platform. Organization-scoped access. No OAuth2 flow. CLI tool for pipeline deployment complements REST API.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Conduit open-source is free to self-host; cloud platform has free tier
pricing.paid_tiers: Cloud platform pricing based on pipeline count and data volume; contact sales
pricing.notes: Conduit runtime is Apache 2.0 open source — free to self-host. Meroxa cloud adds managed hosting, monitoring, and UI on top. Startup-friendly with open-source option.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Documentation covers API basics but less comprehensive than Estuary or Airbyte. Conduit documentation is strong for self-hosted use. Error responses are JSON. Pipeline state management requires polling.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Pipeline creation with same name may fail or create duplicate — check existence first. Connector configuration updates are idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: Conduit plugin ecosystem is smaller than Kafka Connect or Airbyte connector catalog
Pipeline state transitions are async — agents need polling loop
Connector secret handling requires care — stored in platform, not accessible post-creation
CLI tool complements but doesn't replace REST API for all operations

security_score_components.security_notes: SOC2 compliant. Bearer token auth. TLS enforced. Connector secrets encrypted. Open-source Conduit runtime can be audited. Organization-level access control.
